name: Roswell Rudd
abstract: Roswell Hopkins Rudd Jr. (November 17, 1935 – December 21, 2017) was an American jazz trombonist and composer.

interviews.title: Roswell Rudd interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

concerts.label: Roswell Rudd, Trombone, and Badma Khanda's Mongolian Buryat Band

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I was going down working one time with Roswell Rudd, who I used to sing with... I did a, I did a trombone and, and voice thing with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Watrous
about_quotes.evidence: Roswell Rudd and I used to spell each other between the Cinderella Club and the Riviera
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Watrous interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 26, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: Roswell Rudd and John Tchicai, they had a group. It was a quartet with Milford Graves on drums. So I booked them
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: I had the band and I would hire people to play with me... Roswell Rudd and Steve Lacy playing Monk tunes
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Wow. You know Roswell Rudd, the trombone player, mentioned that group. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: Roswell and I and the saxophone player got X’d and they have never had horns since then.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998
